description.value:
Source: Ariadne's Book of Legends - Alpha v1.2, p. 66-71.

Legendary Sorcerer

There are many bloodlines in the Universe that can produce mortals with innate magic–sorcerers, as the scholars called them. A brush with an angel, a romance with a devil, a draconic blessing… these things come upon a person, and by the mysteries of magic, their great-granddaughter is born with healing hands, or a fiery breath. In their rarity, these things are fairly common. You might find thousands of sorcerers in any given nation. The bloodlines that produce Legendary Sorcerers, on the other hand, are unique. There probably isn’t another creature of their kind in living memory. Of course, there is also effort–sorcery isn’t only about the mass of the inheritance, but also the conduit that channels it with more or less efficiency. A Legendary Sorcerer is at the far end of both potential and technique.

These mythical creatures are a shard of something important and forgotten, now returned to its fullest potential. Their power does not come from devotion or discipline or decades of careful learning. It erupts, like a volcano or a sudden storm, from somewhere inside them, growing in intensity across 20 levels. And for those of peerless achievement, it grows to reach what is perhaps its final purpose–surpassment of the ancients. Who’s to say a Legendary Sorcerer isn’t mightier than the dragon who blessed their bloodline eons ago, as a younger tree grows to tower over the one that spawned its seed? Such a thing is difficult to ascertain, given the antiquity in which such bloodline mutations may have been imparted, but when a Legendary Sorcerer devastates a fortress by making a volcano sprout from its foundations, the truth of this question is revealed without the need for hard evidence. The Legendary Sorcerer has joined the mythical halls of those who bless bloodlines, beyond the category of those who use such blessings.

Level 20

Legendary Points

Starting at 20th level, you gain Legendary Points. Your level determines the number of points you have, as shown in the Legendary Points column of the Legendary Sorcerer table. You can take the Recover action on your turn to regain all expended Legendary Points. You can spend these points to fuel various features.

Legendary Resistance

Starting at 20th level, you can expend 1 Legendary Point when you fail a saving throw to succeed instead.

Cantrips

Prepared

— Spell Slots Per Spell Level — 1st 2nd 3rd 4th 5th 6th 7th 8th 9th 10th

Known

Spells

Greater Metamagic

Also at 20th level, you learn 2 greater metamagic options. These work in the same way as regular metamagic options, but they expend Legendary Points instead of Sorcery Points. You learn another greater metamagic option when you reach 22nd and 24th levels.

Legendary AC

At 20th level, if your AC calculation is lower than your level, you can use your level as your AC instead. If you are using your Legendary AC, you cannot further increase your AC in any way, except by temporary AC increases or magical effects lasting 1 hour or less. Other spells and effects that increase your AC only affect your regular AC.

Level 21

Heroic Path Advancement

At 21st level, you choose a Heroic Path you meet the prerequisites for and advance its first step. Each time you gain a level, you can advance a Heroic Path by one step, start a new Heroic Path, or, if specified as an option of the current step you’re on, jump from one Heroic Path into another.

Legendary Score Improvement

When you reach 21st level, and again at levels 23 and 25, increase one of your ability scores by 2 or two ability scores by 1. You cannot raise an ability score above 24 using this feature, unless another feature raised your maximum for that specific ability score beforehand.

Awakened Bloodline

Also at 21st level, your arcane essence reveals a previously dormant facet of its power. Choose one of the following features to be your Awakened Bloodline benefit:

Absorption. When you succeed on a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, you gain 30 temporary hit points.

Primordial Elements. Whenever you deal acid, cold, fire, lightning, necrotic, poison, radiant, or thunder damage, you can change the type of that damage to any other type on the list

Enfeebling Presence. Creatures of your choice within 60 feet of you have a -2 penalty to attack rolls and saving throws while you are conscious.

Arcanophilia. You have a +3 bonus to saving throws against spells and magical effects.

Improved Sorcery

5e. The magic of your bloodline has become everpresent. Your spell save DC is increased by 1, and you have Advantage on the attack rolls of Sorcerer spells you cast. In addition, you can use up to 2 Metamagic options on each spell you cast, and you can use 1 Metamagic option on each of your turns without spending Sorcery Points on it.

5.5e. You are always under the effects of your Innate Sorcery feature.

Level 22

10th-Level Spell

At 22nd level, you learn a single 10th-level spell. You learn an additional 10th-level spell at 24th level. When you start casting a 10th-level spell, you must expend 2 Legendary Points alongside the required action and spell slot.

You learn an additional 10th-level spell when you reach 24th level.

Level 23

Limited Magic Immunity

At 23rd level, your mystical bloodline provides you with innate protection against the arcane. You cannot be affected or detected by spells of 3rd level or lower, and have advantage on saving throws against other spells and magical effects.

Greater Metamagic Master

Also at 23rd level, when you use your Greater Metamagic, you can use up to 2 Greater Metamagic options on the same spell. In addition, when you cast a 10th-level spell, you can use one Greater Metamagic option that costs 1 Legendary Point for free on that spell.

Level 24

Sorcerous Restoration

At 24th level, when you use the Recover action, you regain 4 Sorcery Points.

Magical Sustenance

Also at 24th level, the magical energies flowing through your body sustain you indefinitely. You do not require air, food, or drink, and you cease to age.

In addition, as a bonus action, you can expend any number of Sorcery Points to heal yourself, regaining 10 hit points per Sorcery Point expended. Once you use this bonus action, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

Level 25

Wellspring of Arcana

At 25th level, you reach the cusp of your intrinsic magical power. You do not expend spell slots when casting spells of 3rd level or lower.

In addition, you can use 1 Greater Metamagic option on each of your turns without spending Legendary Points on it.

Greater Metamagic Options

You can use both a Metamagic and a Greater Metamagic option in the same spell. You can only use one Metamagic option on a single spell at a time. When you reach 23rd level, this limitation is removed.

Dominant Spell: When you cast a spell with a duration of Instantaneous, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to make it impervious to abjuration. It cannot be counterspelled and is not nullified by fields of anti-magic or other similar magical effects of Higher tier or lower. Only Epic tier magic can nullify a Dominant Spell.

Metamorphed Spell: When you deal damage with a spell, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to change the damage type to any other of your choice, as the matrix of the spell transforms to suit your will.

Unendurable Spell: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw to resist its effects, you can expend 2 Legendary Points to make one target of the spell fail its saving throw, regardless of the roll. A target can still resist the effects of the spell by using a Legendary Resistance or similar feature.

Overchanneled Spell: When you roll damage for a spell, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to maximize the result of all damage dice. If the spell is being cast at 7th level or higher, you must expend 2 Legendary Points to use this Greater Metamagic Option instead.

Omnified Spell: When you cast a spell with an area of effect, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to duplicate its numerical range. A 30-foot cone, for example, becomes a 60-foot cone, and a 5-foot wide, 30-foot long line becomes a 10-foot wide, 60-foot long line.

Echoed Spell: When you cast a spell that targets only one creature, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to create a phantasmal echo of the spell that lingers around the targeted creature for a minute. As a bonus action, you can cast the spell again originating from the target without expending a spell slot. You can have only one Echoed Spell active at a time. To be eligible, a spell must be of 9th level or lower and incapable of targeting more than one creature at the spell’s current level. For example, magic missile and scorching ray aren’t eligible, but suggestion and dominate monster are.

Incapacitating Spell. When you cast a spell that applies the stunned, paralyzed, frightened, or charmed condition, you can expend 1 Legendary Point to make it affect creatures even if they are immune to those conditions.

source.custom: Original en-US text from Alpha v1.2
